title: Transport & Datadog Agent Communication
description: Flushing finished spans to the local Datadog Agent over HTTP or Unix socket. Covers production and test-mode transport implementations.

actors.name: Test Suite
actors.description: PHPUnit tests that exercise tracing logic; uses DebugTransport and DebugLogger for assertions

actors.name: PHP Application
actors.description: Instrumented application code calling Tracer::flush() at request end

actors.name: Datadog Agent
actors.description: Local sidecar process receiving msgpack-encoded trace payloads over HTTP (DD_AGENT_HOST:DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT) or Unix socket (DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL)

actors.name: C Extension / Background Sender
actors.description: ddtrace C extension that handles msgpack serialization and async HTTP/socket delivery via \DDTrace\flush()

use_cases.name: Flush Traces to Agent
use_cases.description: Tracer::flush() checks enabled flag, logs trace/span counts, then calls Transport::send($tracer) and closes ScopeManager
use_cases.call_chain: Tracer::flush() → check config['enabled'] → logDebug(count,spanCount) → Transport::send($this) → ScopeManager::close()

use_cases.name: Send via Internal (Production) Transport
use_cases.description: Internal::send() delegates to C extension \DDTrace\flush() when DD_TRACE_GENERATE_ROOT_SPAN is false; setHeader() is no-op

use_cases.name: Serialize and Deliver Trace Payload
use_cases.description: C extension serializes spans to msgpack, delivers to Datadog Agent via HTTP POST or Unix socket based on env config

use_cases.name: Capture Traces in Memory (Test Mode)
use_cases.description: DebugTransport::send() calls $tracer->getTracesAsArray() and merges into internal $traces array; no network call made

use_cases.name: Inspect Captured Traces
use_cases.description: Test retrieves in-memory traces via DebugTransport::getTraces() for assertion

use_cases.name: Set Transport Header
use_cases.description: Transport::setHeader(key, value) allows attaching HTTP headers for agent requests; Internal ignores them (no-op), DebugTransport stores them

implementations.Internal: setHeader() is no-op — background sender does not accept headers

use_cases.name: Start Web Server SAPI for Integration Testing
use_cases.description: WebServer::start() selects SAPI implementation based on DD_TRACE_TEST_SAPI env var, starts process, then optionally starts NginxServer for FastCGI
use_cases.call_chain: WebServer::start() → select SAPI (CliServer|PhpCgi|PhpFpm|PhpApache|RoadrunnerServer|OctaneServer|SwooleServer|FrankenphpServer) → $sapi->start() → if isFastCgi(): NginxServer::start()

use_cases.name: Stop Web Server and Await BGS Flush
use_cases.description: WebServer::stop() sleeps 2x flush interval (usleep) to ensure Background Sender delivers traces before process dies, then stops SAPI and NginxServer

use_cases.name: Log Debug/Warning/Error Messages
use_cases.description: DebugLogger captures log records for assertion; supports has(level, message) and hasOnly(level, message) queries

use_cases.name: Check Server Errors
use_cases.description: WebServer::checkErrors() reads PHP error log diff and filters for ddtrace error/warn/deprecated lines, delegates to $sapi->checkErrors() for additional checks

key_classes.name: Internal
key_classes.description: Production transport — delegates to \DDTrace\flush() C extension function

key_classes.name: Noop
key_classes.description: No-operation transport; used when tracing is disabled

key_classes.name: Tracer
key_classes.constructor: __construct($transport=null, $propagators=null, array $config=[]): defaults transport to new Internal()
key_classes.flush_method: flush(): check enabled → logDebug → transport->send($this) → scopeManager->close()

key_relationships: Tracer depends_on Transport (composition, defaults to Internal)
WebServer composes Sapi (one of 8 SAPI types) and optionally NginxServer
WebServer::stop() waits DD_TRACE_AGENT_FLUSH_INTERVAL*2 for BGS to flush

agent_communication.protocol: HTTP POST (default) or Unix Domain Socket

config_vars.DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL: Full URL override; supports unix:// scheme for UDS
config_vars.DD_TRACE_AGENT_FLUSH_AFTER_N_REQUESTS: Flush after N requests (useful for tests)

agent_communication.flush_trigger: End of PHP request lifecycle OR explicit dd_trace_close_all_spans_and_flush() / dd_trace_synchronous_flush()

important_notes: DebugTransport and WebServer live in the tests/ directory — test infrastructure, not production code
Production delivery is entirely handled by the ddtrace C extension (Background Sender / BGS) via \DDTrace\flush()
Internal transport is the default when no transport is injected into Tracer constructor
WebServer::stop() explicitly sleeps 2x flush interval to prevent race between BGS delivery and process shutdown
auto_flush_enabled=1 + generate_root_span=0 is the pattern used by long-lived SAPIs (Swoole, Roadrunner, FrankenPHP, Symfony Messenger)
DebugLogger is used in unit tests to assert that specific log messages were emitted during transport/tracing operations
